place_results.title: CVS
place_results.description: Drugstore chain selling a variety of beauty & health products, plus some grocery & household items.

places.title: MoneyGram
places.address: Inside Cvs, 241 W 57th St, New York, NY 10019

summary.snippet: "Super clean, a large selection of products, friendly staff."
"Very bad service long waiting they I don’t care about Customer Time"
"Super helpful, well staffed, easy access elevator"

most_relevant.description:
This is a nice location and on the larger of CVS's stores in Manhattan. They usually have everything I need.

On my last visit, my cashier was amazing. I feel that she went above and beyond. She was so nice and recommended a larger size of the item I purchased because it was a better deal for me.

I hope someone in mgmt sees this review so they know that she seems like a wonderful person, and she is great with customers, including the people in front of me. I don't see any name on my receipt, but she was cashier #2837785.

This is the kind of person who you want representing your company.  Plus, she was helping a co-worker, but not in a way that distracted her from the shoppers. I usually dont review CVS, but I was compelled to because of her.

Very unprofessional customer service. I asked a simple question about where to find toothpicks, and I was sent to different areas of the store multiple times. After being directed up and down the aisles four separate times and wasting 15–20 minutes looking. I was finally told the item was not in stock.

What made the experience even worse was that there was no apology for the inconvenience. After realizing they didn’t have the item, the employee simply walked away. A little accountability and basic customer service would have gone a long way. Instead, I was left frustrated after wasting my time due to inaccurate directions and poor communication.

The manager at this store, Mary Lee, was super helpful and assisted me in not only locating the product I was searching for, but also showed me how to order online for pick up in store to save extra money on the item. This was the third store I had gone to in search of what I needed, and she was my lucky charm! She also seemed to manage the store expertly and had patience with everyone in a busy store-- from the associates to the customers and everyone seemed happy to be there. It says a lot about management when employees are kind to customers. There were three other customers all waiting for her assistance, and she was not only efficient and helpful, but kind and very cool under pressure. And gave each person her undivided attention.

It's hard to find anything that works in this store. Picking up a prescription? There's always 20+ people in line and only 1 staff member working (and the new self-service screens are useless because you still have to wait for that 1 staff member to physically hand you the prescription). Checking out? There's no one at the register and most self-checkout machines are out of service. Need help? You press the in-aisle customer service button then wait 15+ minutes (if anyone shows up at all). Dropping off expired medication? The disposal bin is always full. Calling the store? No one answers the phone. Need something specific? Products are constantly out of stock.

Overall, this place feels chronically understaffed, poorly managed, and completely unreliable. A total mess.

I visited NYC a few years ago and my most memorable experience as a tourist was how absolutely rude the staff at this pharmacy were. I am not American, so I remember asking a staff member at this store if CVS had any optician services so I can buy a pair of prescription glasses. The staff member bluntly answered no, and when I asked where in the neighbourhood I can find an optometrist, she rolled her eyes and answered that there was a LensCrafters right around the corner. I did not hear her correctly so politely asked her to repeat, to which she effectively yelled at me that there was a LensCrafters right around the corner, and walked away. This encounter happened a while ago and while I don’t expect CVS to do anything about it now, it is quite sad that this was my most memorable encounter visiting NYC. The lack of professionalism and kindness when speaking to customers, especially in an area dense with tourists, sticks!

Very bad experience at this cvs location.. all the products are locked in a glass casing!.. You of to call an employee to open the glass casing each time you wanna access a product.. Then the employees shows up very angry nasty attitude.. I have no time to read & carefully choose the product or products I want... cause the employee is rushing me and rushing to lock the glass casing... Plus my privacy is being invaded i want privacy while I'm shopping!... The employee that opens the glass case shouldn't be all up in my business seeing all the products I'm purchasing.. Zero confidentiality!! This is a major inconvenience!!  Overall bad customer service.. & The African security guard was very rude!

I would give this CVS a 💯 stars if I could. I would like to especially thank the pharmacy staff, they have treated me with such attentiveness and respect. The front of house staff is equally a pleasure to deal with. I left my phone at the self-checkout counter and a staff member saved it for me. I am forever grateful and would highly recommend this CVS. ❤️

I like this location.  The service is consistent and the greeters are always cheerful and upbeat. They add a nice touch when you enter and exit the location. They offer a wide range of products, many of which are on sale. They also have a lot of employees around for assistance without making it feel like you're being harassed. The beauty section is also great and the workers there are friendly and helpful. They do not lock up beauty items. Everything is on display and looks great. There's a large selection and knowledgeable staff. They offer self check out kiosks which are perfect when you're in a rush. They also offer an atm which is convenient. And they have an elevator and escalators for easy access to the second floor. I like it and will continue to visit this location.
